quickStart.title: Get running in 5 minutes
steps: Install from VS Code Marketplace: search 'Kilocode'
Sign in with your Kilocode account (or create one at kilocode.ai)
Open a project folder — Kilocode indexes your codebase automatically
Open the Kilocode sidebar panel (robot icon) and describe a task
Kilocode creates a plan and asks for approval before editing files

dailyWorkflows.title: Start a new task
dailyWorkflows.description: Describe what you want built or fixed in plain English. Kilocode breaks it into steps.
dailyWorkflows.command: Click '+ New Task' in the sidebar or use Ctrl+Shift+K

dailyWorkflows.title: Review the plan before execution
dailyWorkflows.description: Kilocode shows a step-by-step plan before touching your files. Approve, reject, or edit individual steps.
dailyWorkflows.command: Click 'Approve Plan' or 'Edit Plan' in the task view

dailyWorkflows.title: Use Architect mode for big changes
dailyWorkflows.description: Architect mode maps your codebase and plans multi-file refactors with full context.
dailyWorkflows.command: Select 'Architect' from the mode dropdown before submitting a task

dailyWorkflows.title: Chat mode for questions without edits
dailyWorkflows.description: Ask questions about your codebase without triggering any file edits.
dailyWorkflows.command: Select 'Chat' mode — reads code but never writes

dailyWorkflows.title: Run auto mode for simple tasks
dailyWorkflows.description: For clear, low-risk tasks, Auto mode executes without plan approval.
dailyWorkflows.command: Select 'Auto' from mode dropdown — use carefully

dailyWorkflows.title: Apply a diff from the task result
dailyWorkflows.description: After Kilocode generates changes, review the diff and apply selectively.
dailyWorkflows.command: Click file name in task result → review hunk → 'Apply' or 'Reject'

dailyWorkflows.title: Resume a paused task
dailyWorkflows.description: Kilocode pauses when it needs input or hits an ambiguity. Add context and continue.
dailyWorkflows.command: Reply in the task thread and click 'Continue'

dailyWorkflows.title: Switch the model mid-task
dailyWorkflows.description: Change the underlying LLM for the current task without starting over.
dailyWorkflows.command: Click the model selector in the task header

powerMoves.title: Pin a .kilo rules file
powerMoves.description: Add project-specific instructions that apply to every task. Like CLAUDE.md for Kilocode.
powerMoves.why: Persist coding conventions, file layout rules, and banned patterns across sessions.

powerMoves.title: Mention specific files in your task
powerMoves.description: Reference exact files so Kilocode focuses context on what matters.
powerMoves.why: Prevents wasted tokens on unrelated files and gives sharper output.

powerMoves.title: Use the task history panel
powerMoves.description: Every task is stored. Revisit, re-run, or fork previous tasks.
powerMoves.command: Click the clock icon in the sidebar to open task history
powerMoves.why: Useful for iterating on a task that was close but not quite right.

powerMoves.title: Ask Kilocode to explain before editing
powerMoves.description: Use 'explain then ask' phrasing to get a code walkthrough before changes.
powerMoves.command: Prefix your task: 'First explain how X works, then...'
powerMoves.why: Catches misunderstandings before Kilocode starts writing code.

powerMoves.title: Set a token budget
powerMoves.description: Cap how many tokens Kilocode can spend on a task to control cost.
powerMoves.why: Prevents runaway usage on open-ended tasks.

powerMoves.title: Multi-file context with @folder
powerMoves.description: Include an entire directory as context.
powerMoves.command: @src/components/ in your task to include all files under that path
powerMoves.why: Better for refactors where the change spans a whole module.

gotchas.solution: Use Architect mode and review the plan carefully. Add .kiloignore to exclude paths.
Check the sidebar for a pending approval prompt — it won't continue until you respond.

gotchas.problem: Context window exceeded on large codebase
gotchas.solution: Use @file mentions to narrow context. Switch to a model with a larger context window.

gotchas.problem: Auto mode applies changes before review
gotchas.solution: Switch to Architect or Manual mode for any non-trivial task. Auto is for quick one-liners only.
Ensure the file is at the workspace root (same level as package.json or the top-level folder).

gotchas.problem: Diff looks correct but apply breaks the file
gotchas.solution: Review each hunk individually. Kilocode sometimes generates conflicts in files with unusual indentation.

slashCommands.description: Start a fresh task in the current project.
slashCommands.example: /new refactor the auth module to use JWT

slashCommands.description: Ask Kilocode to explain selected code without editing anything.
slashCommands.tip: Great for onboarding to unfamiliar codebases.

slashCommands.description: Generate a plan for a task without executing it.
slashCommands.tip: Review and refine the plan before committing to execution.

slashCommands.description: Revert the last set of file edits made by Kilocode.
slashCommands.tip: Works like Ctrl+Z but for AI edits — shows a diff before reverting.

config.description: Project-level instructions for every task. Markdown format. Supports headings for sections.
config.recommended: Add coding conventions, architecture notes, and paths to avoid editing.

config.description: Paths Kilocode should never read or edit. Same syntax as .gitignore.
config.recommended: Add secrets/, build/, dist/, node_modules/ if not already in .gitignore.

config.description: Default model to use for tasks. Can also be changed per-task.
config.recommended: Use sonnet for most tasks; switch to opus for complex multi-file refactors.

config.description: Whether Auto mode is available for tasks.
config.recommended: Leave false unless you trust the task scope. Architect mode is safer.

proTips: Add .kilo/rules.md immediately on any new project — the earlier the better.
Use Chat mode for exploration, Architect mode for implementation.
Reference specific files with @filename to keep context tight.
Review every plan before approving — the plan quality predicts the output quality.
For multi-step tasks, break them into subtasks rather than one giant prompt.
If a task goes sideways, /undo and re-run with a tighter description.
Kilocode uses the same model throughout a task — pick the right one upfront.
